rules_enforced: Rule 1: No fact_base.json imports or reads
Rule 2: data_source must be dcl or live (demo = FAIL)
Rule 3: No environmental excuses — every test runs
Rule 4: HTTP only — no internal function calls

actual.name: Above-market exec compensation — Meridian
actual.support_reference: Executive compensation analysis vs. industry benchmarks
actual.rationale: Meridian CEO/CFO compensation $18M above market median for comparable firms.

actual.name: Above-market exec compensation — Cascadia
actual.support_reference: Executive compensation analysis vs. industry benchmarks
actual.rationale: Cascadia founder/CEO compensation $8M above market median.

actual.name: Non-recurring: M litigation reserve
actual.support_reference: Meridian 10-K Note 14 — Litigation contingencies
actual.rationale: One-time litigation reserve for patent infringement case settled in Q3.

actual.name: Non-recurring: C delivery center closure
actual.support_reference: Cascadia 10-K Note 8 — Restructuring charges
actual.rationale: One-time charge for Manila delivery center closure and staff transition.

actual.name: Transaction costs — this deal (Meridian)
actual.support_reference: Merger proxy statement — Transaction expenses
actual.rationale: Meridian-side investment banking, legal, and accounting fees for this transaction.

actual.name: Transaction costs — this deal (Cascadia)
actual.support_reference: Merger proxy statement — Transaction expenses
actual.rationale: Cascadia-side advisory and legal fees for this transaction.

actual.name: Related-party leases — C founder properties
actual.support_reference: Cascadia related-party transaction disclosures
actual.rationale: Cascadia leases 2 properties from founder entity at above-market rates; $4M annual excess.

actual.name: Run-rate: new contracts not at full revenue (Meridian)
actual.support_reference: Meridian backlog analysis — contracts signed but not fully ramped
actual.rationale: 3 large contracts signed in Q3-Q4 not yet at full run-rate revenue.

actual.name: Run-rate: new contracts not at full revenue (Cascadia)
actual.support_reference: Cascadia backlog analysis — contracts signed but not fully ramped
actual.rationale: 2 BPM engagements ramping to full headcount in Q1-Q2 2026.

actual.name: Utilization normalization — M Q2-Q3 dip
actual.support_reference: Meridian utilization reports — Q2/Q3 2025 vs. trailing 8-quarter avg
actual.rationale: Q2-Q3 utilization dipped to 73% vs. normalized 78%; add-back for temporary bench buildup.

actual.name: Offshore labor mix shift — C in progress
actual.support_reference: Cascadia workforce planning — offshore migration program
actual.rationale: Cascadia migrating 15% of onshore delivery to offshore; $12M annualized savings at completion.

actual.name: C attrition normalization 18% → 15%
actual.support_reference: Cascadia HR metrics — attrition trending down from 18% to 15% target
actual.rationale: Cascadia attrition at 18% vs. 15% industry norm; excess recruiting/training cost add-back.

actual.name: Bench optimization — consulting
actual.support_reference: Bench utilization analysis — 4500 consultants × cross-deploy model
actual.rationale: Cross-deploying Meridian Partners bench consultants onto Cascadia Process Solutions delivery engagements.

actual.name: Bench optimization — delivery
actual.support_reference: Delivery bench analysis — 4200 FTEs × cross-deploy model
actual.rationale: Redeploying idle Cascadia Process Solutions delivery FTEs onto Meridian Partners project support roles.

actual.name: Corporate function consolidation
actual.support_reference: People overlap analysis — Finance, HR, IT, Legal functions
actual.rationale: Consolidating overlapping corporate functions; reduction based on min(M,C) headcount per function.

actual.name: Vendor consolidation
actual.support_reference: Vendor overlap analysis — 170 overlapping vendors with consolidation savings
actual.rationale: Consolidating overlapping vendor contracts for volume discounts and eliminated redundancy.

actual.name: Technology redundancy elimination
actual.support_reference: Technology stack audit — overlapping SaaS, middleware, and dev tools
actual.rationale: Eliminating duplicate SaaS licenses, middleware, and internal tools post-combination.

actual.name: Cross-sell revenue contribution
actual.support_reference: Cross-sell pipeline — high-confidence ACV × capture rate × margin × ramp
actual.rationale: EBITDA contribution from cross-selling services to the other entity's non-overlapping clients.

actual.name: Integration costs Year 1
actual.support_reference: Integration management office budget — systems, people, branding
actual.rationale: Year 1 integration costs: IT system migration, org redesign, rebranding, change management.

actual.name: Retention packages
actual.support_reference: Retention program — key talent across both entities
actual.rationale: Year 1 retention bonuses for critical leadership and top performers.

results.query: Maestra engagement creation returns valid structure
Give me an overview of where we stand

assertions.actual:
Starting with the cross-sell pipeline — I found a total pipeline value of $272.5 million with 103 named accounts. High-confidence opportunities are $119.2 million. Meridian to Cascadia shows 65 candidates worth $155.1 million, led by F&A Outsourcing opportunities. Cascadia to Meridian shows 38 candidates worth $117.4 million in Data & Analytics and F&A Outsourcing.

**Customer and Vendor Overlap**

34 overlapping customers generating $1.7 billion in combined revenue. MegaCorp Global is our largest shared client at $310 million, which triggers concentration risk thresholds. 170 overlapping vendors with $122 million in combined spend — major consolidation opportunities in cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP), professional services (Big 4), and staffing.

**EBITDA Bridge**

Moving from $1.20 billion reported to $1.86 billion steady-state pro forma EBITDA:

**Combined Organization**

37,900 total corporate headcount with significant function overlap. Consulting/Delivery represents 31,500 people combined.

**IT Integration**

Both systems continue running during the phased integration. Your main conflict is ERP — Meridian uses SAP S/4HANA while Cascadia uses QuickBooks. The intelligence layer will read both during the migration period.

**Quality of Earnings**

Sustainability score of 78.9 (Grade B). The 20 EBITDA adjustments are stable with high confidence levels. Customer concentration is healthy at 13.8% for top 10 clients. Working capital trends are stable with DSO at 13.5 days.

**Key Risk:** MegaCorp Global represents 5.2% concentration risk above typical thresholds.

**Bottom Line:** Strong cross-sell pipeline, significant cost synergies through vendor and function consolidation, stable earnings quality with manageable integration complexity. The $272M cross-sell opportunity plus $400M+ in cost synergies support the deal economics.

What area would you like me to drill into further?

results.query: Walk me through the EBITDA bridge

assertions.actual:
**EBITDA Bridge Walk-Through**

Starting with $1.20 billion combined reported EBITDA, we move through three layers:

**Entity-Level Adjustments (+$228M)**
- **One-time items:** $80M total — Meridian's litigation reserve ($22M), Cascadia's delivery center closure ($35M), and transaction costs ($23M)
- **Normalization:** $93M total — executive compensation above market ($26M), utilization normalization ($45M), related-party lease adjustments ($4M), and attrition normalization ($18M)  
- **Run-rate:** $55M total — new contracts ramping to full revenue ($43M) and Cascadia's offshore labor mix shift ($12M)

**Combination Synergies (+$430M)**
- **Bench optimization:** $135M from cross-deploying consultants and delivery teams
- **Corporate consolidation:** $224M from eliminating overlapping Finance, HR, IT, and Legal functions
- **Vendor consolidation:** $49M from 170 overlapping vendors (AWS, Big 4, staffing)
- **Technology elimination:** $10M from duplicate SaaS and development tools
- **Cross-sell contribution:** $12M EBITDA impact from the $272M pipeline

**Integration Costs (-$125M Year 1)**
- System migration, organizational redesign, and retention packages

**Result:** $1.86 billion steady-state pro forma EBITDA — a 55% increase from reported.

The portal now shows the what-if scenarios. Conservative case drops to $1.64 billion, aggressive case reaches $1.77 billion.

Corporate function consolidation drives the largest synergy at $224M. This assumes you can reduce overlapping roles while maintaining service levels.

Any specific adjustment you want to challenge or understand better?

Starting with the cross-sell pipeline — I found **$272.5 million** total pipeline value across **103 named accounts**.

- **High-confidence opportunities:** $119.2 million (18 Cascadia → Meridian, 4 Meridian → Cascadia)

results.query: Show me the quality of earnings analysis

assertions.actual:
**Quality of Earnings Analysis**

**Sustainability Score: 78.9 (Grade B)**

The combined entity shows strong earnings sustainability with 20 EBITDA adjustments totaling $657.2 million moving from $1.20 billion reported to $1.86 billion pro forma steady-state.

**Key Quality Indicators:**
- **Customer Concentration:** Healthy at 13.8% for top 10 clients — well below risk thresholds
- **Contract Quality:** 8.2 years average client tenure with 31.6% MSA coverage
- **Working Capital:** Stable trends — DSO at 13.5 days, DPO at 10.8 days
- **Margin Trajectory:** EBITDA margin trending from 17.8% to 18.5% over 12 quarters

**Adjustment Confidence:**
- **High confidence:** $145M (executive compensation, litigation reserves, transaction costs)
- **Medium confidence:** $487M (bench optimization, utilization normalization, vendor consolidation)  
- **Low confidence:** $30M (cross-sell contribution, attrition normalization)

**Quarterly Tracking Plan:**
- DSO monitoring (currently stable at 13.5 days)
- Bench cost trending (projected $244M → $252M)
- Margin expansion tracking (18.2% → 18.5% target)

The Grade B sustainability reflects strong fundamentals with some volatility from the high M&A growth component. All 20 adjustments show stable trends with no deterioration signals.

Any specific quality metric you want to examine further?
